place: Eggertsville CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Eggertsville takes its name from Christian Eggert, the town’s first postmaster, whose legacy lives on in the local street names.
Eggert Road, one of the longest streets in the area, honors that same pioneer postmaster and runs north‑south through four municipalities.
Historically, Eggertsville grew as a quiet residential enclave within the larger Town of Amherst, benefiting from its proximity to Buffalo.
The Eggertsville‑Snyder Public Library is often treated as a single community hub, reflecting the close ties between the two hamlets.
Eggertsville is officially part of the Buffalo‑Niagara Falls metropolitan area, linking it to one of New York’s largest urban regions.
Within the Town of Amherst, Eggertsville ranks among five distinct hamlets that share the town’s historic character.
